posts.author: RupeeRiser
posts.text: Built a trading app, SEBI IP restriction has killed it before it even started, need some clarity — This is it.

posts.author: payaljain
posts.text: Built a trading app, SEBI IP restriction has killed it before it even started, need some clarity — From what you described, this looks more like a trade/risk management tool than a fully automated trading system since users are still making the trading decisions manually. The issue is probably the automated order modification through APIs for multiple users, which brokers are now restricting due to SEBI guidelines. The trade sync feature itself definitely shouldn’t count as algo trading. Your dynamic stop-loss idea actually sounds very useful for retail traders.

posts.author: nivas_k
posts.text: Built a trading app, SEBI IP restriction has killed it before it even started, need some clarity — You may share the detailed use case of your platform with us at kiteconnect@zerodha.com , and our team will evaluate and get back to you.

posts.author: Sushen_Sharma
posts.text: Built a trading app, SEBI IP restriction has killed it before it even started, need some clarity — Hi Nivas, thanks for your response. I understand that stop-loss order flow would broadly fall under algo trading. However, would the feature of simply syncing trades from Zerodha to maintain a trading ledger also come under SEBI regulations? Would it be possible to request multi-user access for my app, so that multiple users can sync their trades with a single click instead of manually entering and maintaining their trade journals?

posts.author: nivas_k
posts.text: Built a trading app, SEBI IP restriction has killed it before it even started, need some clarity — This functionality appears to involve order modifications being performed programmatically without manual intervention. In general, scenarios where orders are placed, modified, or cancelled automatically through computer programs or broker API are typically considered under the broader scope of algo trading. Regarding multi-user access, the current workflow of the platform would not align with our existing multi-user functionality.

posts.author: Swapneel_Dey
posts.text: Built a trading app, SEBI IP restriction has killed it before it even started, need some clarity — @nivas_k

posts.author: Sushen_Sharma
posts.text: Built a trading app, SEBI IP restriction has killed it before it even started, need some clarity — Hey everyone, Been building a trading app for past 3 years, solo, and hit a wall I can’t figure out on my own so thought of posting here. The app has two things I am really happy about. One is dynamic stoploss, you set your initial stoploss, and as trade goes in your favour, stoploss keeps adjusting automatically based on rules you define. Second is a sync button that pulls your Zerodha trades directly into the app ledger. Now the problem. Zerodha restricted my Kite app to a single fixed IP, whic…

posts.author: yaaramanil_kumar
posts.text: Built an automated NIFTY options bot on Zerodha Kite — Iron Condor + Butterfly + directional. Sharing what I learned — Over the past few months I built a fully automated NIFTY 50 options trading system on Zerodha Kite Connect. Wanted to share the architecture and get feedback from people who’ve done similar things. What it does: Reads VIX, RSI, MA20, ATR, VWAP every 10 seconds from Kite Picks a strategy automatically based on market conditions: RSI extreme → Iron Condor Strong trend up/down → Long CE / Long PE Low VIX (< 14), no trend → Iron Butterfly Neutral/choppy → Iron Condor Places all 4…

posts.author: Utkarsh_S
posts.text: Built a free tool to track geopolitical News/ Events impact on NSE stocks — Thanks for the feedback! On the confusion - fair point, I’m working on a short onboarding explanation. The simplest way to use it: open any signal, read the causal chain (Trigger → Market Reaction → Investor Sentiment), and check which stocks are flagged as tailwind or pressure. That’s the core of it. On your second question - no the key difference from Google News or an AI news bot is that those tell you what happened. signal.in tries to tell you which of your stocks to care about and why . The goal isn’t news awarene…

posts.author: Utkarsh_S
posts.text: Built a free tool to track geopolitical News/ Events impact on NSE stocks — Hi all, Side project I’ve been working on post-work hours. Sharing here because this community would give the most honest feedback. The problem I kept running into: a global event fires - West Asia tensions, Fed decision, US-China tariffs - and I knew something in my portfolio would be affected, but I had no structured way to figure out what or why. News gives you the event. Nobody gives you the reasoning. What I built: signal.in - an AI pipeline that monitors news and traces the causal path from event → sector → speci…

posts.author: B_b
posts.text: Built a Nifty 50 screener as a student project — is this technically useful? — Hi, I’m a student learning Python and built a screener for Nifty 50 stocks. It scores stocks on 4 factors: RSI (looks for 40-65 range) Price above 50D moving average Price above 200D moving average P/E below 30 Stocks scoring 3+ show as BUY, 2 as WATCH, below as AVOID. Link: https://stock-screener-xmzaccdxnnm4opihvjwa45.streamlit.app My questions for experienced traders: Is this scoring logic reasonable? What would make this actually useful in your daily workflow? What am I missing?

posts.author: cvs
posts.text: Built a free Nifty options simulator after losing money  for 2 years and would love honest feedback from this  community — Why do we need “AI” for any of this? Why will “AI” be better at any of these than traditional systems that already exist and do all of these, and more such? Also, even if “AI” is somehow better at these (not yet established how) If the line of thinking is that with generative-AI, it becomes more affordable to develop such tools, i.e. such tools become ubiquitous, then they no longer represent an edge anymore.

posts.author: RupeeRiser
posts.text: Built a free Nifty options simulator after losing money  for 2 years and would love honest feedback from this  community — Identifying Sweeps, Absorption, Iceberg Orders, Matching liquidity traps using historical Patterns. Anomalies in Index and its Constituents that are Manipulated for Retail Liquidity Sweep. All at a very Granular level and in Real-Time.

That’s why I really respect people who have the ability to navigate the AI tech stack and turn these ideas into real solutions. It feel…

posts.author: cvs
posts.text: Built a free Nifty options simulator after losing money  for 2 years and would love honest feedback from this  community — 2 challenges with this line of thinking - Why will any tool/app be able to decide better with “AI” ? Thinking beyond the immediate, and about second-order effects, is this sustainable, or just becomes an arms-race, i.e. becomes the next lowest bar to clear for everyone, to even try to be viable (+ve returns) and hence an additional cost to simply participate in the market?

posts.author: niftywise

But in a way are not doing the same by making Ai take the decisions similar to the you tube influences and streamers? Just some food for thought.

posts.author: RupeeRiser
posts.text: Built a free Nifty options simulator after losing money  for 2 years and would love honest feedback from this  community — The Western education system has moved away from teaching manual arithmetic. The reason is simple: with calculators available, knowing how to solve every equation by hand is no longer essential. Now, with AI, the need for traditional learning has lost its relevance. The skill that matters most is not understanding every detail, but identifying problems and solving them effectively using AI tools. In India, one pressing problem stands out: Almost all retail traders are buyi…
